engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 206 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 206 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: The DV4TD brought efficient common-rail diesel to the 206, offering strong economy but requiring careful maintenance to avoid turbo and EGR issues.
compatibleVariants: 1.4 HDi Access, 1.4 HDi Active, 1.4 HDi Envy, 1.4 HDi GLX, 1.4 HDi Look, 1.4 HDi S, 1.4 HDi SE, 1.4 HDi Sport, 1.4 HDi Style
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal failure (60k-90k miles)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey cars
Injector seal leaks causing hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV4TD Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Early DV4TD variant code, identical mechanicals to later 8HZ/8HV units with minor ECU calibration differences.
compatibleVariants: 1.4 HDi Entry, 1.4 HDi GLX, 1.4 HDi Look, 1.4 HDi S
commonFailures: Turbo oil feed pipe cracking
EGR cooler carbon buildup
Fuel pump wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HX Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Updated DV4TD code with Euro 4 emissions compliance, featuring revised EGR strategy and improved turbo durability.
compatibleVariants: 1.4 HDi Active, 1.4 HDi Envy, 1.4 HDi SE, 1.4 HDi Sport, 1.4 HDi Style
commonFailures: EGR valve sticking (80k+ miles)
Turbo wastegate actuator wear
DPF-related backpressure on modified units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HZ Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The 1.6 HDi brought stronger performance to the 206 range, but EGR and turbo issues on early units drove significant replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi Active, 1.6 HDi Envy, 1.6 HDi GLX, 1.6 HDi S, 1.6 HDi SE, 1.6 HDi Sport, 1.6 HDi Style, SW 1.6 HDi
commonFailures: EGR valve and cooler failure (70k-100k miles)
Turbo actuator rod wear causing limp mode
Injector coding drift leading to rough running
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6TED4 Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Primary DV6TED4 code for UK 206 models, featuring variable geometry turbo and high-pressure common rail injection.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi Entry, 1.6 HDi GLX, 1.6 HDi Look, 1.6 HDi S, 1.6 HDi SE
commonFailures: EGR cooler internal leakage
Turbo oil return pipe restriction
Swirl flap motor failure on intake manifold
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HY Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output DV6TED4 variant with revised turbo mapping, fitted to sportier 206 trims and SW estate models.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi Sport, 1.6 HDi Style, SW 1.6 HDi Sport, SW 1.6 HDi Style
commonFailures: Turbo VGT vanes sticking under load
EGR differential pressure sensor faults
Injector solenoid wear causing misfires
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HZ Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The robust, naturally aspirated DW8 was the workhorse diesel for early 206 models - simple, reliable, and easy to rebuild.
compatibleVariants: 1.9D Entry, 1.9D GLX, 1.9D Look, 1.9D S, 1.9D SE, 1.9D Style, SW 1.9D
commonFailures: Head gasket failure on overheated engines
Injector pump wear causing hard starting
Water pump bearing failure (timing belt service)
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW8 Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Alternative DW8 code used on certain export and early UK models, mechanically identical to standard DW8 units.
commonFailures: Glow plug relay failure in cold weather
Fuel filter housing seal leaks
Alternator bracket cracking on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for WJY Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The most common 206 petrol engine - simple SOHC design, widely available parts, and affordable rebuilds made it the default replacement choice.
compatibleVariants: LX 1.4, GLX 1.4, XR 1.4, XT 1.4, Roland Garros 1.4, CC 1.4, SW 1.4, 1.4 Access, 1.4 Active, 1.4 Envy, 1.4 Entry, 1.4 Look, 1.4 S, 1.4 SE, 1.4 Sport, 1.4 Style
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing valve damage (60k mile service critical)
Head gasket failure on overheated engines
Coil pack failure causing misfires and rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for TU3JP Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Primary TU3JP code for UK-market 206 models, featuring multipoint injection and standard emissions equipment.
compatibleVariants: LX 1.4, GLX 1.4, XR 1.4, XT 1.4, 1.4 Entry, 1.4 GLX, 1.4 Look, 1.4 S, 1.4 SE
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear leading to belt jump
Thermostat housing cracking causing coolant loss
MAP sensor failure triggering limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for KFW Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Later TU3JP variant with updated ECU mapping for Euro 3 compliance, fitted to facelift 206 models from 2003 onwards.
compatibleVariants: 1.4 Active, 1.4 Envy, 1.4 Look, 1.4 S, 1.4 SE, 1.4 Sport, 1.4 Style, Roland Garros
commonFailures: Crankshaft position sensor intermittent faults
Idle air control valve carbon buildup
Exhaust manifold gasket leaks causing rattles
entries.cta: Get quotes for KFX Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: TU3A variant code with slightly different intake configuration, mechanically compatible with standard TU3JP rebuilds.
commonFailures: Camshaft position sensor failure causing no-start
Fuel pump relay overheating on hot days
Water pump impeller wear at timing belt intervals
entries.cta: Get quotes for KFV Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The 1.6 8-valve petrol offered stronger performance than the 1.4, popular in XR and XT trims, with good aftermarket support for rebuilds.
compatibleVariants: XR 1.6, XT 1.6, 1.6 Access, 1.6 Active, 1.6 Envy, 1.6 Entry, 1.6 GLX, 1.6 Look, 1.6 S, 1.6 SE, 1.6 Sport, 1.6 Style, CC 1.6, SW 1.6
commonFailures: Ignition coil pack failure causing cylinder misfire
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring early replacement
Oil leak from camshaft cover gasket on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for TU5JP Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Primary TU5JP code for UK 206 models, featuring multipoint injection and standard emissions equipment for Euro 2/3 compliance.
compatibleVariants: XR 1.6, XT 1.6, 1.6 Entry, 1.6 GLX, 1.6 Look, 1.6 S, 1.6 SE
commonFailures: Crankshaft pulley bolt loosening causing timing drift
Thermostat sticking open reducing warm-up efficiency
Lambda sensor degradation affecting fuel trim
entries.cta: Get quotes for NFU Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L 16v Petrol
entries.history: The 16-valve version of the 1.6 petrol, fitted to sportier 206 variants, offering smoother power delivery but requiring more precise rebuild tolerances.
commonFailures: Variable valve timing solenoid clogging causing rough idle
Intake manifold swirl flap motor failure
High-pressure fuel pump wear on direct-injection variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for TU5JP4 Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L 16v Petrol
entries.history: TU5JP4 code for higher-spec 206 variants, featuring revised cam profiles and intake tuning for improved mid-range response.
commonFailures: VVT phaser wear causing timing chain rattle
Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting performance
Ignition coil failure under load causing misfire codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for N6A Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The GTi's heart - a robust 2.0L 16-valve engine delivering strong performance, but requiring specialist rebuild knowledge for timing and balance shaft alignment.
compatibleVariants: GTi, GTi 180, RC, CC 2.0, SW 2.0 GT
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure causing valve interference damage
Balance shaft bearing wear causing vibration at high rpm
Oil pump pickup screen clogging on neglected units
entries.cta: Get quotes for EW7J4 Peugeot 206 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Primary EW7J4 code for UK GTi models, featuring revised ECU mapping and intake tuning for the iconic 206 GTi driving experience.
commonFailures: Timing belt idler pulley bearing failure
Oil cooler gasket leaks causing external oil loss
Knock sensor calibration drift affecting ignition timing
entries.cta: Get quotes for RFN Peugeot 206 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 206 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 206's TU3JP petrol and DV4TD/DV6TED4 diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries. While generally reliable with proper maintenance, timing belt failures, turbo issues and head gasket problems can lead to costly repairs. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Peugeot 206 Timing Belt Failure - Why TU3JP & TU5JP Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 206 LX, GLX, XR, XT (1998-2009, TU3JP KFW/KFX & TU5JP NFU)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000-70,000 miles if not replaced at service interval
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner wear or incorrect installation allows belt to jump teeth, causing valves to collide with pistons on these interference engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idler pulleys and water pump before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-mile service interval; only viable if engine undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, reface seats, new gaskets; block inspection required
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block undamaged; risk of hidden piston damage remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt TU3JP/TU5JP with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for damaged engines; eliminates risk of hidden damage; ready to fit

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 206 worth £1,500-£3,500, a full rebuilt engine at £850-£1,950 often represents better long-term value than expensive head repairs. If the belt failed while running, assume valve damage and compare rebuilt options before committing to repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt TU3JP engine prices for your Peugeot 206

problems.h4: Peugeot 206 Turbo Failure HDi - Why DV4TD & DV6TED4 Engines Lose Boost
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-110,000 miles depending on service history and driving style
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed/return restrictions or worn journal bearings cause oil starvation, leading to shaft play, seal failure and loss of boost pressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator, oil seals, clean oil lines; only if turbo core undamaged
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix; risk of repeat failure if bearing wear present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbo cartridge or exchange unit; includes new oil lines and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for moderate wear; 12-18 month typical lifespan

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt diesel engine with upgraded turbo, injectors tested, EGR cleaned, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage or neglected engines; addresses root causes comprehensively

problems.recommendation: If your HDi has lost power with blue smoke or whining noises, the turbo may have already contaminated the engine with metal debris. A rebuilt engine with new turbo and cleaned oil system often costs less than dealer repairs and offers greater peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DV4TD Peugeot 206 diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 206 Head Gasket Failure - Why Overheating Destroys 206 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 206 all variants (1998-2012, all engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles, often triggered by cooling system neglect
problems.rootCause: Coolant loss from failed thermostat, water pump or hose causes overheating; aluminium cylinder head warps, gasket fails, allowing coolant/oil mixing or compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace gasket, reassemble; assumes head undamaged and block not warped
repairOptions.longevity: Rarely viable; most overheated engines have hidden damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Machine head flat, replace valves/guides if needed, new gasket set, pressure test block
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block undamaged; requires specialist machining facilities

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new gaskets, tested cooling system, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for overheated engines; eliminates risk of warped block or cracked head

problems.recommendation: If your 206 has overheated and shows white smoke, coolant loss or oil contamination, assume significant damage. A rebuilt engine at £850-£2,800 typically costs less than dealer head repairs and comes with warranty protection against repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 206 rebuilt engine prices after head gasket failure

problems.h4: Peugeot 206 EGR Valve Failure - Why HDi Engines Lose Performance
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 206 1.4 HDi, 1.6 HDi (2003-2012, DV4TD & DV6TED4 engines)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles, accelerated by short-journey driving and infrequent servicing
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve clogs with carbon deposits, restricting flow; causes rough idle, loss of power, increased emissions and potential DPF blockage on later models.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace EGR valve and cooler; reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early symptoms; may recur if driving pattern unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean EGR, intake manifold, swirl flaps; replace associated sensors and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses broader carbon buildup; improves performance and economy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt DV4TD/DV6TED4 with cleaned intake system, new EGR, tested injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines with multiple carbon-related issues; comprehensive solution

problems.recommendation: If your HDi has persistent EGR faults after cleaning, underlying injector or turbo issues may be accelerating carbon buildup. A rebuilt engine with cleaned intake and tested components often represents better long-term value than repeated EGR repairs.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt DV6TED4 Peugeot 206 engine deals with cleaned EGR system

problems.h4: Peugeot 206 Oil Consumption - Why TU Engines Burn Oil at High Mileage
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 90,000-130,000 miles, worsened by infrequent oil changes and short journeys
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings and valve stem seals allow oil to enter combustion chamber; causes blue smoke, fouled spark plugs, and eventual catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals via cam cover removal; assumes rings still serviceable
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term improvement; does not address ring wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head and sump, replace rings and bearings; requires block honing and precision assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block undamaged; significant labour cost for older vehicles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt petrol engine with upgraded rings, new seals, tested oil pump, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines; eliminates risk of hidden wear; ready to fit

problems.recommendation: On a 206 with 100k+ miles burning oil, a rebuilt engine at £850-£2,300 often costs less than dealer ring replacement and provides a fresh start with warranty coverage against repeat oil consumption issues.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt TU5JP Peugeot 206 engine prices for oil consumption repair

problems.h4: Peugeot 206 Engine Seized - What to Do When Your 206 Won't Turn
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 206 all variants (1998-2012, all engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; often triggered by oil starvation, coolant loss or timing component failure
problems.rootCause: Complete loss of lubrication or catastrophic mechanical failure causes bearings to weld to crankshaft, locking the engine solid.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Seized engines cannot be repaired with minor interventions
repairOptions.longevity: No preventative option once seizure occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Attempting to free a seized engine risks further damage; not recommended
repairOptions.longevity: No reliable intermediate solution for true seizure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine matching original specification, bench tested, 12-month warranty, ready to fit
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable solution for seized engines; eliminates risk of hidden damage; fastest return to road

problems.recommendation: If your 206 engine has seized, replacement is the only reliable option. A rebuilt engine from our network at £850-£2,800 typically costs less than dealer replacement and comes with warranty protection - get quotes today to compare options.
problems.cta: Get emergency quotes for seized Peugeot 206 engine replacement

Compare prices from trusted UK specialists, all supplying engines with upgraded components and a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot 206 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Peugeot 206 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L HDi diesel powered most facelift 206 models from 2003 onwards, offering excellent fuel economy and strong low-end torque for urban driving, though turbo and EGR maintenance is critical for longevity.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 206 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 206 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced in 2005, the 1.6L HDi brought stronger performance to the 206 range while maintaining good economy, but requires careful attention to EGR and turbo maintenance to avoid costly failures.

items.title: Peugeot 206 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 1.9D diesel powered early 206 models with simple, robust mechanics that are easy and affordable to rebuild, though head gasket issues can occur if overheated.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on overheated engines
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 206 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 206 1.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.1L petrol engine offered basic, affordable motoring for early 206 models, with simple SOHC design making rebuilds straightforward and cost-effective for budget-conscious owners.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 60k miles

items.title: Peugeot 206 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common 206 petrol engine, the 1.4L TU3JP powered LX, GLX and XR trims across the model's life, offering a balance of affordability and reliability when timing belt service intervals are respected.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causing valve damage
head gasket failure on overheated engines

items.title: Peugeot 206 1.6L Petrol Engines

items.cta: Compare Peugeot 206 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 206 2.0L Petrol Engines

DW8 (WJY) - 1.9L non-turbo, robust and simple, head gasket failures if overheated

Simple DW8 units easy and affordable to rebuild

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 206 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 206's UK production run (1998-2012), helping owners identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: TU Petrol & DW8 Diesel
years.description: The original 206 launched with simple, robust TU petrol engines and the naturally aspirated DW8 diesel, establishing the model's reputation for affordable motoring.
keyChanges: Peugeot 206 launched in UK with 1.1L, 1.4L and 1.6L petrol engines
1.9L DW8 diesel added for fleet and high-mileage buyers
CC convertible and SW estate body styles introduced mid-cycle
Facelift in 2003 brought revised styling and new HDi diesel options
knownFor: Simple, affordable engines easy for independents to service
Timing belt critical at 60k miles to avoid valve damage
DW8 diesel robust but prone to head gasket issues if overheated
Strong used market support keeps replacement costs low
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998-2003 Peugeot 206 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 206 1.4 engine replacement cost • Peugeot 206 timing belt failure repair • Peugeot 206 1.9D head gasket replacement

years.description: The 2003 facelift introduced the efficient DV4TD 1.4 HDi diesel, transforming the 206's appeal to high-mileage and fleet buyers while petrol TU engines continued.
keyChanges: Revised front/rear styling and interior updates for facelift models
DV4TD 1.4 HDi diesel launched, replacing DW8 for most trims
GTi returned with EW7J4 2.0L petrol engine producing 138hp
CC and SW variants continued with updated engine options
knownFor: 1.4 HDi offered excellent economy but required turbo/EGR maintenance
GTi EW7J4 highly regarded but needs specialist timing knowledge
TU3JP remained affordable to rebuild with wide parts availability
Facelift models dominate current used replacement market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2009 Peugeot 206 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 206 1.4 HDi turbo replacement • Peugeot 206 GTi engine rebuild cost • Peugeot 206 DV4TD EGR valve fix

years.description: The final production phase added the more powerful DV6TED4 1.6 HDi diesel for sportier trims and the SW estate, while petrol variants were gradually phased out.
keyChanges: DV6TED4 1.6 HDi diesel introduced for higher-spec and SW variants
Euro 4 emissions compliance updates across diesel range
Petrol variants gradually discontinued in favour of HDi diesels
Final UK sales ended 2008, with production continuing for export until 2012
knownFor: 1.6 HDi offered strong performance but EGR issues on short journeys
Late-model diesels dominate current replacement enquiries
Petrol engines still affordable to rebuild but less commonly specified
Final-year models often have lower mileage, affecting replacement economics
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2012 Peugeot 206 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 206 1.6 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 206 SW diesel engine cost • Peugeot 206 DV6TED4 turbo repair

faq.h2: Peugeot 206 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 206 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Peugeot 206 engine typically costs £850-£2,800 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour. Get multiple quotes to compare.
keyPoints: TU3JP 1.4 petrol rebuilt: £850-£1,950 supply only
DV4TD 1.4 HDi diesel rebuilt: £950-£2,400 supply only
EW7J4 2.0 GTi rebuilt: £1,800-£4,500 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£900 nationwide
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 206 engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most reliable Peugeot 206 engine to replace with?
items.answer: The DW8 1.9D non-turbo diesel and TU3JP 1.4 petrol are widely regarded as the most reliable and affordable to rebuild, with simple mechanics and wide parts availability across the UK.
keyPoints: DW8 1.9D: robust, no turbo, easy to rebuild, £900-£2,100
TU3JP 1.4 petrol: simple SOHC design, parts widely available
Avoid high-mileage HDi units without full service history
Rebuilt units with upgraded components offer best long-term value
items.cta: Find rebuilt DW8 or TU3JP Peugeot 206 engines with warranty

items.question: What causes Peugeot 206 timing belt failure and how to prevent it?
items.answer: Timing belt failure on TU petrol engines is usually caused by missed service intervals or tensioner wear. Replace belt, tensioner and water pump every 60,000 miles or 5 years to prevent catastrophic valve damage.
keyPoints: TU3JP/TU5JP are interference engines - belt failure bends valves
Tensioner wear is common cause of premature belt jump
Always replace water pump at same time as belt kit
Use OEM-spec belts and genuine tensioners for reliability
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 206 timing belt service or rebuilt engine

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot 206 engine or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: If your 206 is in good body/chassis condition and worth £1,500+, a rebuilt engine at £850-£2,800 often represents better value than scrapping, especially with a 12-month warranty included.
keyPoints: Compare car's market value against rebuild cost plus labour
Rebuilt engines include warranty, reducing future risk
Supply & fit options save time versus DIY or independent fitting
Consider selling with new engine versus scrapping for parts value
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 206 engine replacement cost versus car value

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot 206 and how do I check?
items.answer: Enter your registration above to instantly verify your exact Peugeot 206 engine code via DVLA. Common codes include TU3JP (1.4 petrol), DV4TD (1.4 HDi), DV6TED4 (1.6 HDi) and EW7J4 (2.0 GTi).
keyPoints: DVLA lookup confirms engine code, fuel type and variant instantly
TU3JP, DV4TD and DV6TED4 dominate UK replacement enquiries
Never assume compatibility - always verify code before ordering
Our specialists can advise on cross-compatible codes if needed
items.cta: Check your Peugeot 206 engine code and get compatible quotes

items.question: Do Peugeot 206 HDi engines have DPF and EGR problems?
items.answer: Later 1.4L and 1.6L HDi engines feature EGR valves that can clog on short journeys; DPF was not fitted to most UK 206 HDi models, reducing complexity compared to newer Peugeots.
keyPoints: EGR clogging common on HDi used for short urban trips
Most UK 206 HDi models do not have DPF fitted
Regular motorway runs help keep EGR and turbo healthy
Rebuilt HDi engines include cleaned/replaced EGR components
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 206 HDi engine with cleaned EGR system

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 206 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines can be delivered in 1-3 working days UK-wide. Supply & fit appointments typically take 1-2 days depending on garage workload and parts availability.
keyPoints: Most rebuilt engines dispatched within 24-48 hours of order
Allow 1 full day for professional supply & fit installation
Complex variants (GTi, CC) may require additional adaptation time
Book early to secure preferred fitting slot with specialists
items.cta: Book Peugeot 206 engine supply & fit with UK specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 206 replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a Peugeotmum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options at extra cost
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 206 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Peugeot 206?
items.answer: While engine swaps are technically possible, they require significant modification, may affect insurance and MOT compliance, and are rarely cost-effective versus replacing with the correct code-matched unit.
keyPoints: Code-matched replacement ensures emissions and MOT compliance
Swaps require ECU, wiring and mounting modifications
Insurance companies may decline cover for non-standard engines
Rebuilt original-spec engines offer best balance of cost and legality
items.cta: Get quotes for correct code-matched Peugeot 206 engine replacement
